mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate, exceeding 5%, signals a restrictive monetary policy environment. This increases the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings and reduces credit availability, putting downward pressure on equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:As of June 2023, the FEDFUNDS rate is 5.08%, reflecting an aggressive tightening cycle to combat inflation.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the FEDFUNDS rate is above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from a cyclical low is a reliable leading indicator of a recession. This deterioration in the labor market signals falling consumer demand and corporate profits, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.5pp from its 12-month low
failure_condition: Triggered instances are not followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects broad economic expansion. This directly supports higher aggregate corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive equity market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the most comprehensive measure of economic activity.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between quarterly real GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.10) over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production concurrent with high, persistent inflation indicates stagflation. This environment squeezes corporate profit margins from both rising input costs and falling final demand, leading to sharply negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month change < -1.0% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was a historically poor period for equity market performance.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained, strong job creation indicates underlying economic momentum and robust aggregate demand. A high 6-month moving average of nonfarm payrolls additions supports corporate revenue growth and thus positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The monthly employment situation report is a key market-moving data release.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the 6-month change in PAYEMS for 20-day forward returns is consistently below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A negative surprise in monthly retail sales data signals an unexpected weakening in consumer spending. This immediately lowers revenue forecasts for consumer-facing sectors and causes a negative short-term sentiment shock for the broader market.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for roughly two-thirds of U.S. GDP.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the central bank is actively tightening policy, any upside surprise in inflation data increases the probability of further rate hikes. This raises the discount rate on equities and leads to negative returns as the market prices in a more hawkish Fed.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, months with high CPI prints are not followed by statistically significant negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: The housing market is a key interest-rate sensitive and forward-looking sector. A sharp year-over-year decline in housing starts signals falling investment and consumer confidence, preceding broader economic weakness and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Residential fixed investment is a component of GDP that typically turns down before recessions.
failure_condition: Periods where HOUST falls more than 20% YoY are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns on average.
